name: New England Clam Chowder
recipeIngredient: 20 top neck clams, shelled, 1 chopped, strained and juice reserved
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, melt the clarified butter over medium heat. Add the celery, leeks, and onions. Cook for six minutes, stirring occasionally.
2. Add the diced potatoes. Stir in the white flour until well combined.
3. Simmer for five more minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
4. Add the chopped clams, juice, and reserved liquid from the clams. Continue simmering for one minute.
5. Stir in the heavy cream and water. Cook until heated through, about 10-15 minutes total.
6. Season with salt to taste.
7. Remove the pot from heat and let sit for a few minutes before serving. This steeping process enhances the flavor of the chowder. If desired, add more water to achieve the preferred consistency.
